text:
Objective
---
Quick experimental workbook to translate regional GPU-hour prices and utilization profiles into $/1M-tokens estimates (hosted, pay-as-you-go baseline). This is a lightweight scenario lab (not the final canonical model) intended for infra/product tradeoff conversations and to seed a canonical spreadsheet.

High-level approach (formula)
---
1) baseline_tokens_per_gpu_hour = throughput_tokens_per_sec * 3600
2) gpu_hours_per_1M_tokens = 1_000_000 / baseline_tokens_per_gpu_hour
3) base_cost_per_1M = gpu_hours_per_1M_tokens * list_price_per_gpu_hour_by_region
4) final_cost_per_1M = base_cost_per_1M * (1 / effective_utilization) * infra_overhead_multiplier * (1 - cache_hit_rate) * reserved_discount

Assumptions used in this lab (conservative midpoints)
---
- Representative GPUs and steady-state throughput (tokens/sec):
 - H100: 12,000 tps
 - A100: 6,000 tps
 - L4: 2,500 tps
- I...

title: Replica skew and adaptive interconnect scheduling
preview:
Summary:
This notes doc captures experiments and hypotheses around replica-level load skew interacting with the interconnect (NVLink/NVSwitch/PCIe) and NCCL collective choices. We observed recurring tail-latency and throughput loss when one or two replicas in a replica-group become heavier due to request imbalance o...
text:
Summary:
This notes doc captures experiments and hypotheses around replica-level load skew interacting with the interconnect (NVLink/NVSwitch/PCIe) and NCCL collective choices. We observed recurring tail-latency and throughput loss when one or two replicas in a replica-group become heavier due to request imbalance or KV-cache replay spikes. Aim is to record tests, quick reproductions, and candidate mitigations for scheduling and runtime heuristics.

Background / motivation:
- Redwood serving runtime relies on a mix of tensor-parallel (TP) and pipeline-parallel (PP) shards across multiple GPUs. For multi-replica deployments we expect identical workload per replica, but in production skew happens (prefix-cache replays, cache-miss storms, user-bursting).
- When one replica is heavier the interconnect and collective algorithms expose imbalance: NCCL allreduce/allgather waits, increased ho...

text:
Quick goal: capture a concise event timeline + commands tried + hypotheses for the Dedicated GPU pool preemption/eviction spike on 2025-01-12. This is a scratchpad — messy.

Symptoms observed:
- Sudden backlog of pending inference requests; tail latency jumped x10 for ~18m.
- Dedicated GPU pool showed repeated node add/remove cycles (~6 scale events in 20m).
- Several pods saw PodEvicted / NodeLost and FailedScheduling with 'insufficient nvidia.com/gpu' messages.
- Customer reported 502s around the same time (correlated to pool id: dedicated-eu-west-2-a).

Timeline (local times UTC):
- 2025-01-12 03:10: metric alert: "dedicated-eu-west-2-a.available_gpus" dropped below 4 (alert rule id 231).
- 03:11: autoscaler scale-up triggered (requested +2 nodes).
- 03:12: new nodes registered but many pods Pending due to initcontainers/driver probe flapping.
- 03:17: node kernel logs show multipl...

text:
Purpose
-----
Notes for a small, portable evaluation harness that devs can run locally or in CI to validate model behavior (generation, chat, structure, embeddings). Focus: reproducible runs, simple manifest, CLI ergonomics, and a lightweight golden-output ledger that can be versioned alongside code.

Motivation / problem statements
- We run large eval suites in infra but small quick checks are painful to author/run locally.
- Need a single manifest format that describes input prompts, expected outputs (optional), tolerances, metric hooks, and seed/runtime constraints.
- CLI should make it trivial to: run single-case, run a manifest, diff outputs vs golden, collect metrics, and emit machine-readable reports for CI.

Principles
- Lightweight: no heavy infra required to start. Single binary (py CLI or go) with pluggable backend adapters.
- Deterministic where possible: explicit seeds, t...

title: Attention prefetch tolerance and cost-model notes
preview:
Summary:
This is a working note exploring the notion of a "prefetch tolerance" — the window (in tokens / microseconds) where aggressive KV/prefix prefetching improves tail latency and unit-cost, versus when it increases register pressure, causes bank conflicts, or forces kernel deoptimizations (paged -> flash fallba...
text:
Summary:
This is a working note exploring the notion of a "prefetch tolerance" — the window (in tokens / microseconds) where aggressive KV/prefix prefetching improves tail latency and unit-cost, versus when it increases register pressure, causes bank conflicts, or forces kernel deoptimizations (paged -> flash fallbacks) that hurt throughput. The goal is to capture qualitative heuristics and a simple cost model we can use in the scheduler to decide prefetch aggressiveness per request/queue.

Why this matters:
- We see cases where enabling prefix/KV prefetching for medium-length chats reduces average latency but increases 95/99p tail because fusedops run with higher register pressure and start evicting tiled scratchpad pages.
- Autoscaling and routing decisions depend on predicted cost per token; prefetching knobs can swing that by 5–30% depending on workload mix.
- Current heuristics a...

title: GPU pool resiliency & degenerate-burst handbook — wireframes + backlog
preview:
Purpose
This is a working sketch + backlog for a new console surface focused on GPU pool resiliency and handling degenerate burst patterns. The intent is to reduce operator cognitive load when pools behave unexpectedly (spikes, noisy-tenants, slow-starting models), provide clear fallbacks, and make the tradeoffs of ...
text:
Purpose
This is a working sketch + backlog for a new console surface focused on GPU pool resiliency and handling degenerate burst patterns. The intent is to reduce operator cognitive load when pools behave unexpectedly (spikes, noisy-tenants, slow-starting models), provide clear fallbacks, and make the tradeoffs of burst/reservation actions explicit.

Context and motivation
- We've got a class of incidents where small, frequent short-lived requests create lumpy KV-cache/truncation churn that starves long-running streams. Existing pool views show utilization and reservations but don't make "resiliency state" or fallback behaviors visible.
- Dev teams ask for a predictable way to: 1) isolate noisy tenants, 2) temporarily route low-priority traffic to lower-cost model variants, and 3) simulate burst scenarios to evaluate SLAs.
- This doc takes UX-first approach: wireframes for a Pool Res...

title: Adaptive delta slotting and epochal hinting experiments
preview:
Summary

This doc captures a set of small-scale experiments exploring two complementary techniques to reduce KV cache fragmentation and lower tail latency under mixed workloads: (1) adaptive delta slotting — a dynamic variable-slot allocator that adjusts slot grain on per-key-size distributions, and (2) epochal hint...
text:
Summary

This doc captures a set of small-scale experiments exploring two complementary techniques to reduce KV cache fragmentation and lower tail latency under mixed workloads: (1) adaptive delta slotting — a dynamic variable-slot allocator that adjusts slot grain on per-key-size distributions, and (2) epochal hinting — a lightweight metadata epoch per request that biases retention/eviction without heavy global counters. The goal: materially reduce memory waste (measured as unusable internal fragmentation) while keeping eviction cost and allocation latency low enough for 99.9% SLOs.

Background and motivation

We’ve been seeing two recurring issues: (a) high internal fragmentation from uniform slot sizes when serving a mix of short and long KV blobs (e.g., chat contexts vs. embeddings), and (b) eviction thrash when tenant request patterns change rapidly (bursty prefix access). Existi...

Purpose:
Quick, reusable \"playlets\" (short script + micro-deck snippets) for sellers and SEs to run pilots, manage canaries, and execute graceful fallbacks during early production migrations. This doc is an authoring sketch — lean on it to generate a 6–8 slide deck + one-pager cheat card for field use.

Audience:
- Solutions Engineers (primary)
- Account Execs / Platform Sellers (secondary)
- Customer SRE/Platform contacts during pilot handoff

Core thesis (single line):
Make the pilot safe, measurably low-risk, and fast to iterate by packaging: a) narrow success criteria, b) simple rollback/fallback tactics, c) transparent economics, and d) an SE checklist that fits inside a 10-minute conversation.

Recommended slide flow (6 slides + appendix):
1) 30s hook: pilot outcome + what success looks like (latency SLO, cost target, sample user story)

text:
Status: draft — notes, TODOs, and experimental commands ahead.

Context and goals
- Target: customers that cannot reach public registries (air-gapped / sealed VPCs).
- Deliverable: a repeatable, testable offline bundle + operator pattern that:
 - populates an internal OCI registry or registry proxy,
 - provides a verified artifact index and checksums,
 - exposes a compact terraform module interface so Solutions Eng can seed registries during install,
 - supports safe rolling upgrades with minimal downtime.

Design constraints and assumptions
- No direct internet egress from customer cluster during install/upgrade.
- Operators/agents run in an admin network segment with controlled upload channel (sneaker-net, S3 to transfer station, or customer-provided bastion).
- Primary artifacts: container images, Helm charts, K8s CRDs, operator manifests, model artifact tarballs (optional), and li...

title: Microburst latency fingerprint notes
preview:
Context and why this doc exists:

We observed several short-duration latency spikes (microbursts) in the hosted fleet during early Nov 2024 that show up as narrow but high-magnitude p99 excursions in traces for a subset of workloads. This note collects an initial fingerprint of those microbursts, a hypothesis matrix...
text:
Context and why this doc exists:

We observed several short-duration latency spikes (microbursts) in the hosted fleet during early Nov 2024 that show up as narrow but high-magnitude p99 excursions in traces for a subset of workloads. This note collects an initial fingerprint of those microbursts, a hypothesis matrix, sampling instructions, and suggested mitigations to test. Unlike the broader long-tail studies, these spikes are transient (100ms-3s windows) and correlate with bursty arrival patterns and certain batching states.

Summary of observed behavior (high level):
- Frequency: ~2-5 microbursts per hour per pod for affected pods (traffic pattern: bursty chat workloads, multi-turn).
- Duration: median 450ms, 90th 930ms, max ~2.6s (measured in e2e traces).
- Affected quantiles: minimal movement at p50 and p95; p99 and p999 show pronounced spikes.
- Rough impact: for a cohort of 320...

text:
Top-level goal: get a 6–8 week pilot with HavenBank (mid-sized regional bank, core: FIS, 2.6M retail accounts). Focus: contextualized customer support assistant + KYC intake templating to reduce handle time and TTR for first contact resolution.

Quick thesis / ROI hook (for outreach):
- Reduce average contact center handle time by 18–25% through prefix caching + short-context templates.
- Cut repeated prompt calls to core systems by batching and local caching — estimated token reduction 30% vs naive API.
- Avoid data egress / PII risk by offering Private deployment (VPC) or Dedicated with KMS integration.
- Pilot success metric: 15% reduction in AHT (avg handle time) or 3000 saved agent-hours/month for the pilot segment.

Account notes (public & scraped intel):
- HQ: Minneapolis; ~2.6M retail; 12 regional branches in Midwest.
- Tech stack: core = FIS; cloud: AWS (us-east primary), MFA...

text:
Purpose:
Quick personal scratchpad for running a shadow cohort (mirror traffic) and rehearsing rollback scenarios before we flip any live routing. Not an org playbook — more a bootstrapping checklist + gotchas list I use to run sims and hand off to on-call/owner.

Context and when to use this:
- Use when rolling a new model binary or new runtime change that *touches hotpath* (KV layout, attention kernel, cache eviction).
- When we want to validate upstream production traffic without exposing users to output changes (mirror-only) AND validate rollback automation works (dry-run + simulated failure).

Goals for the run:
- Validate that shaded/mirrored requests don't increase p99 latency above baseline by > 10% under typical load.
- Confirm rollback automation (traffic reconfiguration + unpin) completes within SLO (target: 90s for config flip + 5m to stabilize).
- Prove pinning/unpin TTL ...

title: Stream scratchpad thrashing and CUDA workspace sizing notes
preview:
Summary:

This note captures debugging iterations and hypotheses for a recurring class of OOM and high tail-latency incidents we observed in streaming/scoring workloads on multi-stream GPUs. Symptoms: intermittent OOMs during steady-state traffic (not growth spikes), repeatable higher-than-expected peak resident mem...
text:
Summary:

This note captures debugging iterations and hypotheses for a recurring class of OOM and high tail-latency incidents we observed in streaming/scoring workloads on multi-stream GPUs. Symptoms: intermittent OOMs during steady-state traffic (not growth spikes), repeatable higher-than-expected peak resident memory, and long pauses in request completion correlated with cudaMalloc/cudaFree and kernel scheduling stalls.

High-level hypothesis (current):
- Workloads that use many short-lived ephemeral buffers (per-request scratch/workspace) create high allocator churn across CUDA streams. This churn combines with large library (cuBLAS/cuDNN) temporary workspace allocations to fragment internal arenas and force slow cudaMalloc/cudaFree paths.
- In particular, convolution/cuBLAS workspace size calls can vary by invocation (shape/algorithm choice) and may request transient allocations t...

text:
Context:
Quick working notes from discovery / kickoff with Aegis Pharma (R&D informatics). They are exploring a private deployment of Redwood for hallucination-sensitive summarization of clinical trial notes and protocol ingestion. High sensitivity: PHI in unstructured notes, strict auditability, need for on-prem or VPC-isolated control plane with KMS/HSM integration. This doc is a free-form thoughtstream — not polished.

Call recap (2024-09-15):
- Attendees: Ethan (Redwood SE), Sofia (Redwood Solutions), Priya (Redwood PM), Dr. Martin Chen (Aegis Data Lead), Rana Kapoor (Aegis SecOps), Alejandro Ruiz (Aegis Platform).
- Duration: ~60m. High-level ask: private deployment option that can run nightly batch summarization and real-time triage assistant for safety team.
- Immediate goal: small proof-of-concept (PoC) to validate latency and PHI handling.

title: Routing Tenancy Decision Checklist

It focuses on: isolation boundaries, tenant routing semantics, control-plane responsibilities, multi-region failover, and ...
text:
Purpose:
This is a practical decision checklist and set of annotated scenarios to help platform engineers choose routing and isolation defaults across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments. It focuses on: isolation boundaries, tenant routing semantics, control-plane responsibilities, multi-region failover, and operational runbook tasks for onboarding and incident response.

Intended audience:
Platform engineers, SREs, solutions engineers, and security reviewers who make trade-offs between latency, cost, compliance, and reliability when selecting routing models for a customer or workload.

High-level decision flow (one-page):
1) Ask: Does the customer require physical compute isolation or just logical isolation?
 - Physical isolation OR dedicated capacity for throughput/SLA -> Consider Dedicated or Private.
 - Logical isolation (per-tenant namespaces, separate creds) acceptable ->...

title: Straggler mitigation experiments — replica hedging + jittered prefetch (scratch)
preview:
Goal:
- Quick, informal log of experiments aimed at reducing p99 tail caused by a small fraction of long-running model requests (stragglers). Focused on two lightweight mitigations: replica hedging (dispatch a second replica after delay) and jittered prefetch (start nvtx-prefetch for KV cache with randomized offset)...
text:
Goal:
- Quick, informal log of experiments aimed at reducing p99 tail caused by a small fraction of long-running model requests (stragglers). Focused on two lightweight mitigations: replica hedging (dispatch a second replica after delay) and jittered prefetch (start nvtx-prefetch for KV cache with randomized offset).

Background/context:
- Running 8x A100 pool, steady-state QPS ~120 for the test shard. Baseline request profile: 90% short (<=128 tokens), 9% medium (129-512), 1% long (>1024). Long requests cause KV cache churn / kernel stalls and inflate p99 by ~3x.

Experimental design (quick):
- Workload: replay production arrival traces sampled for 60m window (diurnal mid-peak), preserving interarrival variability.
- Baseline config (A): single-replica per request, no prefetch.
- Hedging config (B): issue hedged request to second replica if primary not started producing tokens after ...

title: GPU SKU allocation scenario planner
preview:
Purpose:
This working doc defines a scenario-driven SKU allocation and reorder-policy model for Redwood's GPU fleet across regions. It is intended to complement the existing inventory and procurement trackers by: 1) making trade-offs explicit when lead-times spike, 2) allocating constrained SKUs to the highest-value...
text:
Purpose:
This working doc defines a scenario-driven SKU allocation and reorder-policy model for Redwood's GPU fleet across regions. It is intended to complement the existing inventory and procurement trackers by: 1) making trade-offs explicit when lead-times spike, 2) allocating constrained SKUs to the highest-value workloads, and 3) producing clear reorder triggers and cadence for procurement ops.

Context / why this is new: current trackers are delivery-focused (orders, commits) but lack a reusable scenario layer to tell finance, SRE, and product how we should shift SKU mix under supply stress. This is the team-level operational playbook for Q2–Q4 2024.

Key assumptions (summary):
- Demand baseline: 20k GPU-hours/day (global) as of Jan 2024, projected growth 18% q/q under baseline product growth signals.
- SKU classes considered: A100-80GB (training heavy), A100-40GB (mixed), H100-8...
